auto_approve.mechanism: kiro_mcp_auto_approve: Kiro supports per-server or per-tool auto-approval configuration to suppress confirmation prompts

enterprise_management.mechanism: Kiro does not document organization-level MCP management

env_var_expansion.mechanism: Kiro MCP configuration does not document environment variable expansion

marketplace.mechanism: Kiro does not have an in-IDE MCP marketplace

oauth_support.mechanism: Kiro MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication

resource_referencing.mechanism: Kiro does not document MCP resource referencing

tool_filtering.mechanism: kiro_mcp_disabled_tools: Kiro supports per-server tool disable configuration to control which MCP tools are available to the agent

transport_types.mechanism: Kiro MCP transport types not documented beyond basic MCP support

providerExtensions.name: autoApprove per-server tool list (or wildcard)
providerExtensions.description: Each mcpServers entry can include an 'autoApprove' field: either an array of specific tool names to auto-approve without user confirmation, or the string '*' to auto-approve all tools on that server. This is per-server granularity, more fine-grained than a global approve-all setting.

providerExtensions.name: disabledTools per-server list
providerExtensions.description: Each mcpServers entry can include a 'disabledTools' array to explicitly suppress specific tools from a server without disabling the entire server. Complements 'autoApprove' for fine-grained tool permission management.

providerExtensions.name: disabled flag per server entry
providerExtensions.description: Each mcpServers entry can include a 'disabled: true' flag to deactivate a server without removing its configuration. Allows toggling servers on/off without editing config structure.

providerExtensions.name: Remote server support (url + headers)
providerExtensions.description: In addition to stdio servers (command + args), Kiro supports remote MCP servers configured with a 'url' field (HTTPS, or HTTP for localhost) and an optional 'headers' map for authentication tokens. Env var expansion uses ${VARIABLE} syntax in config values.
